message.content: Lea here with my full CSS/frontend review of the Waiting For implementation. ## Overall Assessment The CSS is well-structured, follows design tokens consistently, uses logical properties throughout, and stays within `@layer components`. BEM naming is consistent with our existing components. The templates use semantic HTML with proper accessibility attributes. Focus management JS follows the established pattern from other pages. Good work. ## BLOCKING Issues **1. Missing `` element for date display** (`waiting_for_item.html:6`) Currently: ```html {{ item.date_added }} ``` Should use semantic `` element: ```html {{ item.date_added }} ``` The `` element is semantic HTML that helps screen readers and machines parse dates. This requires the template struct to provide both a machine-readable ISO datetime string and a human-readable display string. The US-GTD-5 acceptance criteria says "Each item shows: title, who/what it's waiting on, **date added**" — the `` element ensures this is properly conveyed. Every other date-displaying page in the web standards world uses `` for this purpose. **2. Empty state text does not match user story** (`waiting_for.html:59`) Currently: ``` Nothing to follow up on. Items you're waiting on from others will appear here. ``` US-GTD-5 specifies: ``` Nothing pending from others. When you delegate something or need to follow up, add it here. ``` I flagged this in my design review feedback. The user story text includes an actionable instruction ("add it here") which is better UX. Please use the specified copy. **3. Missing `waiting_on` pill-badge styling** (`waiting_for_item.html:4`, `main.css:2230-2233`) The `waiting_on` person/entity text is currently displayed as plain muted text: ```css .waiting-for-item__person { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--color-fg-muted); } ``` This should use the pill-badge pattern consistent with how we display context tags (`.next-action-item__context`) to visually distinguish the metadata from the title: ```css .waiting-for-item__person { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--color-fg-muted); padding: var(--space-xs) var(--space-sm); background-color: var(--color-gray-100); border-radius: var(--radius-full); } ``` Without this, the person text visually blends into the title, making it harder to scan the list quickly. Visual distinction of metadata is important for the "see everything they are waiting on in one glance" success metric. ## Minor Issues **4. Add form layout differs from established pattern** (`main.css:2164-2196`) The add form uses a stacked vertical layout (`flex-direction: column`) with two separate groups, while every other add form in the app (inbox, next-actions, projects) uses the horizontal `flex-wrap: wrap` pattern with `align-items: end`. This inconsistency is noticeable when navigating between pages. I understand the two-field form motivated this choice (and the vertical stack does work), so I won't block on it — but consider whether aligning with the established horizontal row pattern would be worth it for visual consistency. Not blocking. **5. `color: var(--color-fg-subtle)` on date** (`main.css:2238`) `--color-fg-subtle` maps to `gray-400` which is 3:1 contrast. Per our token documentation, this is "NOT for body text" — it's acceptable only for large text or UI components. The date text at `font-size-xs` is small text, so 3:1 may not meet AA for that size. Consider using `var(--color-fg-muted)` (gray-600, ~7:1) instead, which comfortably passes AA at any text size. Not blocking since the text is supplementary, but worth fixing. ## Nice-to-have **6. Edit form labels are visually hidden** (`waiting_for_edit.html:11,21`) The edit form uses `class="visually-hidden"` on both labels ("Title" and "Who or what"). This is fine for screen readers, but since there are two input fields stacked vertically, sighted users might not know which field is which (the placeholders help, but placeholders disappear on focus). Consider making at least the "Who or what" label visible since the two fields look identical otherwise. Not blocking — the placeholders are adequate for now. ## Summary - **3 BLOCKING**: `` element, empty state copy, person pill-badge styling - **2 Minor**: add form layout consistency, date contrast - **1 Nice-to-have**: visible edit form labels Everything else looks good — the CSS architecture, token usage, logical properties, focus management JS, dashboard integration, HTMX patterns, and accessibility attributes are all solid.
